label: UptimeRobot
description: Monitor website and service uptime, check status, and retrieve alert data from UptimeRobot.

form.title: Connect UptimeRobot
form.subtitle: Paste your UptimeRobot API key to start pulling monitor status and uptime data.

methods.label: Main API Key
methods.description: Use your account-level main API key from UptimeRobot's API Settings. Grants read/write access to all monitors, alert contacts, and account data.
methods.how_to:
## Find your Main API Key

The main API key gives Anton full access to your UptimeRobot account, including all monitors and alert contacts.

### Steps

1. Log in to [UptimeRobot](https://uptimerobot.com) and click your profile/avatar in the top-right corner.
2. Select **My Settings** from the dropdown.
3. Scroll down to the **API Settings** section.
4. Under **Main API Key**, click **Show/Hide** to reveal the key, then copy it.
5. Paste it into the **Main API Key** field below.

### Notes

- The main API key has both read and write permissions — keep it secret.
- If you only need read access (e.g., for reporting or dashboards), use the **Read-Only API Key** method instead.
- You can regenerate the key in the same panel if you suspect it has been compromised; doing so will invalidate the key stored in Anton.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: Main API Key
fields.description: Found in UptimeRobot → My Settings → API Settings → Main API Key.

methods.label: Read-Only API Key
methods.description: Use a monitor-specific or account-level read-only API key. Safe for dashboards and reporting — cannot create or modify monitors.
methods.how_to:
## Find your Read-Only API Key

UptimeRobot provides a read-only API key that restricts access to GET-style operations — ideal when you only need to query monitor statuses and uptime ratios.

### Account-level read-only key

1. Log in to [UptimeRobot](https://uptimerobot.com) and click your profile/avatar.
2. Select **My Settings**.
3. Scroll to **API Settings**.
4. Under **Read-Only API Key**, click **Show/Hide**, then copy the key.
5. Paste it into the **Read-Only API Key** field below.

### Monitor-specific read-only key

1. From the dashboard, click the monitor you want to expose.
2. Open its **Edit** panel.
3. Scroll to the **Monitor-Specific Read-Only API Key** section and copy the key shown there.
4. Paste it into the field below.

### Notes

- Read-only keys cannot create, update, or delete monitors or alert contacts.
- Monitor-specific keys only return data for that one monitor.
- Regenerating a key in the UptimeRobot UI will invalidate what Anton has stored — re-paste the new key to restore access.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: Read-Only API Key
fields.description: Found in My Settings → API Settings → Read-Only API Key, or inside an individual monitor's edit panel for a monitor-scoped key.

fields.name: monitor_id
fields.label: Monitor ID (optional)
fields.description: If using a monitor-specific read-only key, enter the corresponding monitor ID here so Anton can scope requests correctly. Leave blank for account-level read-only keys.
